name: Dependency Audit
description: Audits project dependencies for vulnerabilities, outdated packages, and licence compliance

checks.fail: ℹ️  No production dependencies — skipping audit

checks.command: pnpm audit --audit-level=moderate 2>&1 | tail -5 || npm audit --audit-level=moderate 2>&1 | tail -5 || echo 'audit-not-available'
failRecommendations: Run `pnpm audit --fix` or `npm audit fix` to auto-remediate
Review remaining issues manually; consider `npm audit fix --force` with caution

checks.command: pnpm outdated 2>&1 | head -30 || npm outdated 2>&1 | head -30 || echo 'outdated-check-unavailable'
checks.fail: ⚠️  Some packages may be outdated — see findings for details

checks.prompt:
Review the package.json for the following:
1. Dependencies that are known to have breaking changes in newer major versions
2. Packages that have been deprecated or transferred to new names
3. Large transitive dependency footprint concerns (e.g. shipping the entire lodash)
4. Any licence incompatibilities between MIT, Apache-2.0, and GPL packages

Flag any findings as CRITICAL only for GPL licence violations or actively exploited CVEs.
